aid: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Alngith.
ain: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Ainu.
air: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Airoran.
and: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Ansus.
ani: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Andi.
dag: symbol. Symbol for decagram, an SI unit of mass equal to 10 grams.
daring: verb. present participle and gerund of dare
ding: noun. Very minor damage caused by being struck; a small dent or chip.
drag: noun. Resistance of a fluid to something moving through it.
drain: noun. A conduit allowing liquid to flow out of an otherwise contained volume; a plughole
gad: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Gaddang.
gadi: noun. A cushioned throne used by Indian princes.
gag: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Gagauz.
gaging: verb. present participle and gerund of gage
gan: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Gan.
gar: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Galeya.
gid: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Gidar.
grid: noun. A rectangular array of squares or rectangles of equal size, such as in a crossword puzzle.
grin: noun. A smile in which the lips are parted to reveal the teeth.
grind: verb. To reduce to smaller pieces by crushing with lateral motion.
nadir: noun. The point of the celestial sphere, directly opposite the zenith; inferior pole of the horizon; point of the celestial sphere directly
nag: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Naga Pidgin.
raid: noun. A quick hostile or predatory incursion or invasion in a battle.
rain: noun. Condensed water falling from a cloud.
ran: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Riantana.
rand: noun. The border of an area of land, now especially marshland.
rani: noun. The wife of a rajah.
ranid: adjective. Of or pertaining to the true frogs.
ria: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Reang.
rid: verb. Followed by of: to free (oneself or someone, or a place) from an annoyance or hindrance.
rig: noun. The rigging of a sailing ship or other such craft.
rin: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Nungu.
ring: noun. A solid object in the shape of a circle.
